commands.author: Alix-007

target.title: fix(control-ui): WebSocket connects to wrong gateway when two share an origin
target.body:
Closes #97636

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with two OpenClaw Gateways served under different basePaths on the same origin (e.g. `https://example.com/gateway-a/` and `https://example.com/gateway-b/`) would find that Gateway B's WebSocket connected to Gateway A's backend. The symptom is deterministic: any gateway page that has no saved settings of its own picks up the most-recently-saved sibling gateway's URL and opens its WebSocket there instead.

## Why This Change Was Made

`persistSettings` was writing every save to two keys: a URL-scoped key (`openclaw.control.settings.v1:wss://…/gateway-a`) and the legacy unscoped key (`openclaw.control.settings.v1`). `loadSettings` fell back to the unscoped key when the scoped key was absent. Because all gateways on the same origin share `localStorage`, the unscoped key always contained the URL of whichever gateway last saved—making the fallback read a cross-gateway pollutant.

Two focused changes fix this:

1. **`loadSettings`**: before accepting a fallback from the unscoped key, compare the stored `gatewayUrl`'s basePath scope against the page-derived URL. Reject the fallback when scopes differ so a sibling's settings are never adopted.
2. **`persistSettings`**: stop writing to the legacy unscoped key. Each gateway already writes a URL-scoped key; the unscoped write served only as a pollution vector.

## User Impact

Operators serving multiple gateways under one origin with path-based routing will no longer see a gateway page connect its WebSocket to a sibling gateway's backend. Each gateway page now derives its connection URL exclusively from its own basePath, regardless of which other gateways have been opened in the same browser profile.

## Evidence

Two new unit tests in `storage.node.test.ts` cover the regression and the write-side fix:

```
✓ does not adopt the gateway URL from a sibling gateway sharing the same origin
✓ does not write to the legacy unscoped settings key when saving
```

Full suite result (22/22):

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  22 passed (22)
  Duration  1.87s
```

The existing "ignores and scrubs legacy persisted tokens" test is updated to use a matching basePath pathname (the real legacy-migration scenario) so it continues to verify token scrubbing without relying on the now-rejected cross-basePath fallback.

_AI-assisted (Claude Sonnet, logic verified and tests written by author)_
target.author: alix-007
